messages.pattern: Must be alphanumerical and not contain any spaces or special characters

fieldData.author: From our friends in Tirana, Albania
fieldData.description:
We would like to share with you an update about the ministry among the people in need.

Our volunteers have been visiting the poor people, have encouraged them and have prayed for their needs. We have been giving food packages to them. People felt the love of God and they are grateful for taking care of them while they suffer in poverty.

Thank you so much for your precious prayers and support that is bringing hope to many people in need!
fieldData.name: Albania

fieldData.description:
Feeding ministry in Caracas, Venezuela.

Thank you for your support.
fieldData.name: Venezuela

fieldData.description:
Greetings from Albania!

We would like to share with you an update for the ministry among the people who suffer in poverty.

We have helped with food packages many poor people in Tirana. Our volunteers have been visiting the poor families, the abandoned elderly people and the orphans and have encouraged them to trust God. They have prayed for their needs. People want to learn more about God and are grateful for the help.

Thank you so much for being a huge blessing for the poor people!

fieldData.author: From our friends in Albania.
fieldData.description:
We are so thankful to God and to High5Give5 for providing for many poor people.

Many people in need have been helped with food packages in Tirana and the surrounding areas. Volunteers from the church have served among the poor. They have prayed and shared the Gospel with them. People were open to learn about Jesus.There are no words to describe the hope that covered people when they received food.

Thank you so much for your precious prayers and your support. You are a huge blessing for a lot of people in need.
fieldData.name: Albania

fieldData.description:
This month, the children and families attending the feeding programs learned about the Jesus' childhood and the beginning of his ministry. We focused on Jesus being the only way to the father.

Approximately100kids and parents were fed weekly. We have also began to give the parents a small bag of bread for their families.

A group from YWAM's leadership school came to help in the feeding program. We visited new tenement homes and evangelized the neighborhood, inviting more children and families to church and the feeding program. We have had the opportunity to reach new kids and families with the gospel. Several of the teenagers from the neighborhood were able to join a Youth week at the church. They learnt about evangelism, played games and had times of prayer and worship.

Thank you for your support and prayers.

fieldData.description: Feeding ministry in Kenya - Thank you for your support
fieldData.name: Kenya

fieldData.description:
Here is the report for June 2024. This month, we fed approximately 80 kids and parents weekly (400 meals this month). This month we began teaching about God's promises in the Bible and in our lives.

Many of the kids who attend the feeding program are traveling over summer break. We are using this opportunity to reach new families in our community.

The group of teenagers attending has continued to grow. We have seen them learn about God, begin to attend church and the youth group. We continue to pray for a true encounter with God in each of their lives.

We thank you for your support and prayers!
fieldData.name: Colombia

fieldData.author: From our friends in Tirana
fieldData.description:
Due to your precious support the poor people, the lonely elderly people and orphans have been helped with food packages. Our volunteers have been visiting the people who suffer in poverty and have encouraged them to trust God and to lay all their worries and needs to Him.

People are so grateful for all the care and help. They are open for the Gospel.

Thank you so much for your precious support! You are a huge blessing for the people who suffer in poverty.

fieldData.description:
Approximately 100 kids and parents weekly (400 meals this month). We have also been giving them a small snack as they arrive, since many don't eat before coming.

This month, we continued teaching about Jesus' parables and the salvation message through them. The kids and parents have learned about God's character and his love for us.

We had several teams from the US and Colombia, supporting us this month. They were a great help and blessing for the ministry. The volunteers have also grown in their commitment and responsibility to serve wholeheartedly.

We continue to pray that the feeding program can be a space where kids can experience Gods love and presence.

We thankyou foryoursupport andprayers!

fieldData.name: Armenia

fieldData.description:
In April, we fed approximately 100 kids and parents weekly (400 meals this month).This month, we have explored Jesus' parables and how they apply to our lives. The kids and parents have learned the importance of hearing and putting God's word into practice.

The number of teenagers coming to the feeding program has grown so much and they have begun attending the Saturday youth meetings and Sunday church services. We have seen true fruit in their lives as they come to know God personally.

We celebrated Children's Day with a special day full of games and crafts. We also had a drama explaining the parable of the mustard seed and how God uses small beginningsto do great things. Through this drama, we encouraged the kids to share the gospel and serve others, as their small beginning.

We thank you for your support and prayers!
fieldData.name: Colombia

fieldData.description:
We have enrolled 5 boys in a skilling program with the church. They are learning book making, sound mixing and playing instruments.

We have resettled 5 other boys back to their parents in the last 3 months. We have helped 4 others go back to school.

Not directly paying school fees but talking them into leaving the street and going back to school. We have registered more than 15 salvations among these kids.

We have also gotten an opportunity to engage adults and women who are homeless as well.

All this is made possible because of your generosity.

Many blessings.
fieldData.name: Uganda

fieldData.author: From Johnathan
fieldData.description:
Thank you for funding the monthly food bank that provides for orphans with no parents, children and families living in extreme poverty.

Thank you High5 for your extreme generosity and compassion for the poor. You fed over 200+ people this month that were in survival mode.

On behalf of all the children and families you fed thank you for providing milk, eggs, bread, vegetables, cooking oil, oats, and much more!

Warmly,

Johnathan.
fieldData.name: India

fieldData.description:
Feeding and food distribution in Albania.

We would like to share with un update about the ministry among the people in need.

Due to your support many poor families, elderly people and orphans have been helped with food packages. Our volunteers have meet them, have encouraged them and have prayed for their needs. People have been so grateful forthehelp thatwegave. They are open for the gospel and some of them are attending the church meetings.

Please find attached some pictures from the ministry.

We appreciate your support a lot. Thank you so much for being a huge blessing for many people who suffer in deep poverty!
fieldData.name: Albania

fieldData.description:
This month we have fed approximately 100 kids and parents weekly.We have started receiving groups of around 30 adults and children, from an Organization called CAMMI. They are Venezuelans receiving government support to travel to other cities or countries. They attend the Saturday morning program where they hear the gospel, have a hot meal, and take a shower before continuing their trip.

This month, a barber school volunteered haircuts for the boys who attended the feeding program. About 15 boyswere able to get a free haircut. A large group of teenage boys and girls have started attending more regularly. Several of them have started coming to the youth group and Sunday services.

This month, during "Semana Santa" (easter break) we had a 3-day kids VBS for ages 7-15. Around 60 kids attended each day, from 10-4, and heard about God's character through lessons about the Trinity. They received a hot meal and two snacks throughout the day. We did arts and crafts, dramas, sang songs, and taught lessons through games and puzzles. We had many new volunteers from the church who joined us and were able to show the kids God´s love.

Thank you for your continuous support and prayers!
fieldData.name: Colombia

fieldData.author: From Johnathan
fieldData.description:
Thank you so much for the food distribution that you made possible in India.

This month we were able to get fresh carrots, potatoes, onions, chapati flour, eggs, milk, oil, oatmeal, bread, vitamin biscuits, and apples.

You made this possible for these struggling families in total six villages benefiting from this food distribution support that you graciously gave.

May God continue to bless you and the work that you put your hands to at high five. We also pray a special prayer that God would bless all of your donors for their compassionate hearts to give selflessly at High 5.Johnathan
fieldData.name: India

Feeding ministry in the Philippines

From Lucille,

Hi Bettina and High5Give5 team,We had been so blessed to have teams come and helped us in our kids ministry. It was a special treat for the kids being carried and hugged. Not just receiving the Word of God but as well as affection.

The kids went home being emotionally and spiritually filled. Their hunger was also filled from the yummy food that we prepare for them.Thank you so much High5Give5 for your continued support to our feeding program! May the Lord bless you more abundantly. ‭‭

" The Lord has done great things for us, and we are filled with joy." Psalm.126:3

fieldData.name: Philippines

fieldData.description:
Thank you so much for the support to purchase food bank supplies that were distributed on February 7th 2024.

The people of this region of India live in extreme poverty due to lack of work and educational opportunities. The benefactors of the food you graciously provided are from over 7 villages we thankfully serve.

Your support to feed these less fortunate children and families is such a huge component to the multifaceted holistic approach we have in helping these people. We just finished building a new English school this week. And your food will help nourish the minds of the children while they earn their education. We value your partnership and invite you to come see what you make possible in Orissa, India as a valued and cherished partner of Love Never Fails Ministry.Thank you for your support.
fieldData.name: India

fieldData.author: From Medellin
fieldData.description:
This month, the children and families attending the feeding programs learned about the Israelites in the desert. We focused on trusting God's provision, protection, and the 10 commandments. Approximately 150kids and parents were fed weekly.We have had missionary teams from Germany and the US with us this month.

They have helped us pick up the kids in the morning, sing and dance during praise and worship, and teach lessons in new and exciting ways. Also, they have been visiting the Venezuela tenement homes in our area, sharing the gospel through movies, dramas, and testimonies.We handed out school supplies kits to the kids who started school this month and were in need. The kits included backpacks, notebooks, and writing materials. Many kids are not enrolled in school, so we will start encouraging them to come to the City of Refuge during the week to spend time with them and help them continue learning at home.

Also, we gave grocery store gift cards to 10 families who have been faithful in attending the feeding program and whose children have shown an interest in learning about the Lord. The families attended church service, and after, we spent time with them. We spoke about the importance of relying on God for all our needs (not only financial) and encouraging them to continue being faithful.Thank you for your support and prayersbeginning this New Year.
fieldData.name: Colombia

fieldData.description: The children we feed in karamoja often are hungry and naked. They live in the harsh conditions of karamoja where the rains can take six months if not more without raining. Most of the parents used to keep cattle and depended on cattle for everything. But because of insecurity, many of them lost the cows as well. The Community development officer extends her appreciation to High5 for the support you are offering to the children of the community.The Local council 3 leadership, LC3 is the equivalent of the mayor of the community here. He thanks High5 for feeding their children, and appreciates the Church for teaching discipline and literacy to the children. The porridge and the snack is always enough except on days when kids go beyond 800.Thank you so much for making a difference in the lives of our children.
fieldData.name: Uganda

fieldData.description: Feeding ministry in Kenya. Thank you for your continued support.
fieldData.name: Kenya

‍
fieldData.name: Venezuela

fieldData.description:
Feeding ministry in #Medellin #Colombia. Thank you for your support.

Last month we fed approximately 130 kids and parents weekly. The children and families attending the feeding programs have heard the gospel through lessons, games, and crafts. This month we taught about Jesus's birth and the events surrounding it.

Several of the Venezuelan moms continue attending church and have joined discipleship groups. Also, we started picking up Venezuelan families from a government tenement home in the city's center. Around 30 new kids started coming with their fathers and mothers.

We had a fair-themed Christmas party where the kids participated in games and answered trivia questions to win and choose their prizes. We gave out toys, books, clothes/accessories, and candy.  We also had hot dogs, popcorn, candied apples, and ice cream, to treat the kids to something special.
The moms spent the day making “Hallacas” a traditional Venezuelan Christmas dish, so that they could feel more at home. The moms received books, kitchen supplies, and essentials for their babies.

We also started our annual year-end fundraiser to provide school kits, groceries, and other items to families in need. We will be handing out the supplies in January, after a Christmas break for the workers.

Thank you for your support and prayers.
fieldData.name: Colombia

fieldData.description:
During Christmas time we helped with food packages many widows, orphans, elderly people and poor families. We also gave them Christmas pamphlets and calendars with Bible verses. They were so thankful for giving them the opportunity to celebrate at the end of the year by having food on their tables in these hard times. Our volunteers shared God's love with them. They learned that God takes care of them.
In the oncologic hospital and the psychiatric hospital in Tirana we gave bags with food and gifts. Patients who were fighting for their life and were suffering  from illnesses, felt the love of God and learned about the greatest Gift. They had questions about Jesus. Our volunteers prayed with them. They expressed their gratitude to our volunteers with tears in their eyes.

We appreciate a lot your support in all these years for the ministry among the people in need!  You are giving hope to many poor people through your support. God's love is changing their lives.
Thank you so much that you will continue the support for 2024!

May God richly bless you!

fieldData.description:
This is a very poor community that lives on the banks of open sewage in the slums of Nagpur. They are desperately in need of this help.

27 families were given essential emergency relief bags which consisted of two bags 90% food and the other 10% we're hygiene supplies such as toothpaste soap hand soap dishwashing soap and hand sanitizer.

And I'll behalf of them I want to say thank you to High5 and your team for what you make possible to these children and their families all in the name of Jesus.

fieldData.author: From our friends in Turkey
fieldData.description:
Latest from our friends in Turkey

The virus slowed, and now has surged again here as soon as people started going out freely again. We continue to help the very poorest people with the white food bags, flour, eggs and chicken.

Your gift was very timely, and we were able to bless many people from it.

We have been able to give food sacks to a few refugees in town here, and 30 to another town.

The children living near our home are still memorising Bible passages, but not as actively as at first.

God is good, and we are able to share food in Jesus name, which is wonderful. Thank you again for your gift of funds.

‍

Food distribution to 100 families in th slums in Cebu. Food bags Include 3 kilos of rice, 320 grams milk, 1 kilo of brown sugar, coffee, 1/2 kilos of noodles, shampoo and bath soap .  30 families were fire victims, 70 families from our feeding program locations.

fieldData.name: Philippines

fieldData.description:
Greetings from Albania!

The situation caused by Covid-19 is very dark. Fear, depression, panic and despair have filled the people. Unemployment is at a very high level. A lot of people are without food.
Many people are calling us from different areas of Albania asking for help. Some people who called us were feeding their babies with water and sugar and their older children were going to bed hungry and empty stomachs as they had no food and no income to buy food. Others were collecting wild plants in the mountains to feed their children. The government doesn't have a proper system of providing help for all the people who are in need.

We are really happy and grateful that God provided, so that many families and hungry children were helped.
We gave food packages to the people in need in the area of Tirana and Durres. Believers from the church visited the families. We shared the love of God with them. People were so thankful. With tears in their eyes they were saying that when nobody was caring for us you answered and provided.
41 families were helped x 24.1 $ per food package.
Praise the Lord for his blessing in the life of these families.

Thank you very much for your precious support!

‍
fieldData.name: Albania

fieldData.author: From Mike Gerard
fieldData.description:
Dear High5Give5,

We can’t thank you enough for your continued support to King’s Bridge International throughout this very difficult year. Due to the global pandemic, our usual overseas mission trips have been halted, but of course the basic needs of the poor we serve, continue on.

As the world’s resources become more scarce, your giving is a sign of God’s love and provision to those who need it most.

Thankfully, covid-19 infections in Uganda remain relatively low, and haven’t reached the remote areas we serve, like the Children’s Orphanage in Wakiso, which feeds up to 150 children a day. You have made it possible for us to fill the food storehouse with staple foods, such as beans, rice and posho, as well occasional special “meat” meals for holidays. As travel restrictions to Uganda (currently all but closed) lighten up, we are planning to resume our evangelistic mission trips by early summer of 2021. Our mission remains reaching out to the poor, and spreading the life- changing message of Salvation through Jesus Christ.

In the midst of these troubling times, I believe that very soon we will have an unprecedented window of opportunity to spread the gospel as never before.

Jesus said, “Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, for they are already white for harvest!” (John 4:35)

We are praying for wisdom and vigilance in this exciting and crucial hour. Thank you again for your kind and generous support.

In Christ,

Mike Gerard

Director, King’s Bridge International

‍
fieldData.name: Uganda

fieldData.name: Greece

fieldData.description: Just started feeding in 3 schools in Kenya. Thank you for making it possible.
fieldData.name: Kenya

fieldData.author: From Djurdjevo
fieldData.description:
Local authorities in Djurdjevo stopped us from distributing food. We need to find an alternative solution as we feed 80 children in this area. We continue to feed and bring God's word to 140 children and adults in Kucura and 40 children and 40 adults in Zmajevo. In Zmajevo, the children attend Sunday school. In our church, our school project continues with 50 children. We are in need of school material for them. Each week, we serve about 300 warm meals.

Thank you for your continued support

‍
fieldData.name: Serbia

fieldData.description:
For a couple of months now, the Feeding Center, Come, Play and Learn, has been operating thanks to the support.

The goal is to improve the quality of life and the spiritual and emotional health of the children through a food program, we have been able to observe the social environment that generates various types of problems, and how this environment affects the school, emotional growth and spiritual activities of the children.

Talking with several children and parents, we realized that many children live in homes full of problems. Some of these children take care of their younger siblings, and do not eat enough. Home hygiene is also a concern and it is part of our ministry action to improve this environment through teaching.

We appreciate the support to carry out this effort for change.

fieldData.author: From Ocala, Florida
fieldData.description:
Every Friday afternoon a team of volunteers go out to deliver 150 adult meals and 50 children’s meals to the people on our route, along with bags of groceries, breads and pastries.

Every Friday morning two other teams make the preparations: one team cooks and packs the meals, the other team packs the groceries, breads, and pastries.

We always have a time of prayer with the people before we distribute the food.

We also take special requests like bicycles and furniture, and we grocery shop for some people who are really sick and have no transportation.

Blessings to you!

Thank you for your continued support

fieldData.author: From our friends in Albania
fieldData.description:
We would like to share with you about the ministry among the poor and the needy people.

On the Orphan’s Day, on 20th of May, we organized a special event for the orphans in one of the areas in Tirana. We cooperated with the municipality and a dancing academy in Tirana. Some children from the dancing academy danced together with the orphans and brought great joy in the sad lives of the orphans in Tirana. Our team played with the children, made face painting and gave gifts and aid to them. They enjoyed the time together so much and were grateful for our help. Their faces were full of joy and hope.

We have been helping many elderly people and families in need in the area of Tirana and in the villages around. Our team has visited, has met and has given food packages to the poor people. Also, we have encouraged them and have shared the love of God with them. Hope has filled many lives. They are grateful that we have been near them, especially in these difficult and hard times. Many have opened their hearts for the gospel. Praise the Lord for providing and for covering these people with His love and hope.

Please find attached some pictures from the ministry.

Thank you so much for your precious support! You are such a blessing for many elderly people, orphans, children and families in need.

‍

fieldData.author: From our friends in Medellin
fieldData.description:
We continued with weekly feeding for 200 kids. We also did a big outreach Thursday and Friday and grocery despatch.

We have been doing outreach in the bronx (an area heavily populated by addicts and prostitues). Now twice a week instead of once, and we are starting to see fruits! Men, women and teens coming into restoration programs.

We also launched this month weekly outreach in the local inquilinatos (project housing). We can't work with all of the houses in our community but we are asking Jesus to show us which ones we can adopt.

We are trying to focus our feeding program efforts and humanitarian aid efforts on these selected homes.

Trying to be more focused, go deeper and increase discipleship efforts.

God is good, it is an amazing season of ministry for me as a Christian and missionary. Also learning to walk with Jesus as husband, it has been a special time. Sometimes there is lonliness, but He has been very present.

Thank you for uour continued support.

fieldData.name: Armenia

fieldData.author: From Pastor Alexander
fieldData.description:
Many people we first met at the beginning of the war were convinced that the war would be over soon and they would all be back home by the end of the summer. That was hope for all of us at the time. I will never forget the conversation we had with a Christian psychologist. We have been discussing how we help people get over the physiological trauma of the war and the evacuation. That was the main issue, the problem to address here and now. But for her, that was a minor issue; as a doctor, she looked into the future and foreseen more significant problems - depression, desperation, hopelessness, and suicidal inclinations. She looked at us and said: these are inevitable, and it will hit people hard. An invisible enemy that will attack from the inside, the battle of the mind.

We have been so overwhelmed with traumatic episodes HERE AND NOW that looking into the future was not something that would have come easily; we have prayed and thought, how can we be ready, what can we do to help people, but we have never been expecting such an enormous and an overwhelming wave of hopelessness. For many refugees, mainly women and mothers, this had come with the realization that their kids would have to be in school. We are getting hundreds of calls from mothers sobbing with hopelessness and frustration, fear and overwhelming anxiety. There are hours and hours of comforting and praying, but a smile is hidden. In the last several weeks the, words like “I have nothing to live for,”; “I have got no energy to face another day,”; “ I am so tired that I have no desire to see another day,”; “why am I still alive, it would have been better to die from the bombings in Ukraine.”.

With the time and procrastination of the war, with fading hope of the war ending soon, there are fewer and fewer people available to help people like them. I remember conversing with one of the mothers about their time and adjustment in the new country. I remember her saying to me: “as much as we are grateful for all the help we have been given, for the use of translations in the schools, for helping us understand the message in the church, there is need more than understanding a message, we need to be able to talk to someone, pray and worship with someone in the language we all understand. We need a time of fellowship.” Thus, we have to pray, asking the Lord for guidance, the place and time for people to help one another. Suddenly I realized, perhaps more than ever before, that the primary NEED, the church's main calling, IS fellowship.

While facing the challenges in the “safe” land, there is a non-stop challenge in the war zone. The flood of people seems to have no end; every week, two twenty-seater vans leave Ukraine with refugees. People that come to the House of Hope from Kherson, Mykolaiv, Primorsk, and Odesa with tears in their eyes say: “we were told that you would not cast us out, and you are the only one who would help us.” Non-stop flow of people, hurt, stories of atrocities they have gone through, pain, and anguish. At times hearing the stories of their lives and feeling like you have walked their alley of death with them, there are days I can’t write a word. My journaling or communication is minimized to almost none. THERE IS STILL WAR and vicious and painful and brutal WAR.

The war leaves people broken, hopeless, empty, and hungry. Simple and essential food is still a luxury for many people in southern Ukraine, even more so in the remote villages that had been hit hard with corona restrictions and now the war. The nonstop messages from the government of Ukraine to prepare for the most brutal winter ever do not help much. Older adults wish to die before the winter, not wanting to suffer from cold and starvation. Such a weird, weird thing, this is the country that has been invited to join the EU. The crisis of war, famine, and now energy crisis is hitting people in the southern part of Ukraine in an unthinkable way.

“IF YOU CAN’T FEED A HUNDRED PEOPLE, THEN FEED JUST ONE.” –SAID MOTHER TERESA. These words are so relevant to what we see now taking place around us. Not only in the southern part of Ukraine but here locally as well. How WONDERFUL that God placed the church and good people as agents of help, love, compassion, and mercy for the hurting people.

Thank you for your prayers. Because of your prayers, hundreds of women and children and finding refuge in the EU, hundreds are helped locally in Ukraine, and the team of people volunteering is still just amazing and keeps on moving. They are my heroes of faith - six months of persistent pain, hurt and hard work, but they are still moving, still going.

It has been a tough month for us in the midst of all that we have been going through; my mom went to be with the Lord 12 days ago. Having to go to the funeral and flying to Latvia from various places was difficult, but we are humbled and very thankful to those who knew and prayed for us. People often ask me - “how are you holding up,” and there is always a challenge, what do I say, the truth or polite? The polite - I am Ok or the fact - I am not well yet, but I will be.

Today, we celebrate our wedding anniversary, the first one without my mom's phone call and prayer of blessing over us. We have decided to go for a short getaway in Bergen this weekend to be alone, reflect on His mercies, and celebrate His faithfulness.

Thank you for your continued support.

‍
fieldData.name: Ukraine

fieldData.author: From Eric, Managua
fieldData.description:
Adriana Lopez

Adriana de los Ángeles López.

8 years old.

Currently, she lives with her father and her four siblings, because her mother is one of the thousands of Nicaraguans who are immigrating to the United States through "coyotes" trying to cross the border illegally and still achieve the American dream, hoping to leave behind the economic crises of Nicaragua, but, the cost to pay is also leaving behind their young children.

On a regular basis, Adriana spends most of her time with her older brother (15 years old), since the two little ones are under the care of a neighbor during the day, since their father goes out to work as a loader and driver of a food delivery truck with shifts where he must sleep away from home.

The 4 siblings are taken by this compassionate neighbor to one of the Feeding Centers that are supported by Your Ministry.

For Adriana, it is synonymous of joy, fun, and care, when she comes here.

She tells us that they have had food shortages in their home in the past, so the Feeding Center has served to meet that need.

"I miss my mom, but, I know she is gone because she will help us, and one day we will all be together again."

"I also know that my dad is not with us because he has to work" "He also misses my mom a lot."

The flow of migrants leaving Nicaragua and trying to cross the Rio Bravo (Border with the United States) has become unstoppable; just between March and April of 2022, a dozen of them died in the attempt.

According to the border patrol data, last year 2021, almost 90,000 Nicaraguans tried to cross the border without documents.

We know that there are many Adrianas alone in Nicaragua, waiting to be back again with their fathers or mothers.

Thank you for your continued support.

‍
fieldData.name: Nicaragua

fieldData.author: From our friends in Tirana
fieldData.description:
Greetings from Albania!

Hope you are doing well.

We wanted to share with you about the blessed ministry among people in need.

We are so thankful to God and to High5Give5 for providing for many poor families.

Many abandoned elderly people, widows and families in need were helped with food packages in Tirana and surrounding areas. Our volunteers have served among the poor. We visited the families, gave the food packages, shared the Good News with them and prayed for their needs. People were so grateful and filled with hope. They learned that God loves them.

Thank you so much for all your prayers and your precious support. You have been a huge blessing for many people who suffer in poverty.

May the Lord's blessing, protection and grace be with you.

Thank you for your continued support.

The photos are from last Saturday. We took 90 kids and single moms to the park to play. Some of these kids have never been off their street. The most special part was seeing struggling women poor and beaten by their husband's laughing like children and enjoying a day of play. God is good:)

Thank you for your continued support.

fieldData.author: From Brindisi
fieldData.description:
We are grateful to God for 2021: a year full of challenges due to Covid 19 restrictions but at the same time blessed by His hand on our ministry.

God used High5Give5 to help so many families.

2022 started with a great effort to share the Gospel in a new and fresh way with the people in need.

During the month of January we served more than 30 families with a box of long-lasting food, fresh vegetables and fruits, products for the hygiene.

We are also continuing to deliver food to the families that are in quarantine because of Covid: the majority of them don't have stable jobs and they barely receive a monthly support from the Italian government.

A father that we've been serving for a while came to collect the box: he was desperate because his young little girl started losing her hairs from one day to another and they don't know the cause.

Out volunteers encouraged him to have faith and they promised our prayers.

Thank you for your continued support.

fieldData.author: From Ocala, Florida
fieldData.description:
We go out by van weekly to serve the needy. This is a father with his daughter. They live in a mobile home park. We gave the father groceries, pastries, hot soup and a sandwich. We give special bag lunches to the children.

We gave groceries to Ruby as well as a $25 gas card for travel to Florida University at Shands in Gainesville, which is an hour away. She’ll have tumors removed from her brain to alleviate seizures.

We also visit seniors in rehab facilities, assisted living and nursing homes. We pray for them, comfort them and give them communion. We also request prayers for them.

We thank you for your generosity, and we pray for you and your amazing ministry.

fieldData.author: From Pastor in Kachilitwa
fieldData.description:
WELL - completed

Kachilitwa is a village in Baringo county Kenya. It is arid area where some droughts have lasted even more than a year. When the drought comes, food security is huge problem. There are no crops and cattle dies.

The only permanent source of water is a small stream nearby, but the water is not very clean and at times it dries up.

There is a Full Gospel church who is active in serving the community and evangelizing those villages around which don’t yet have churches.

Next to the church is a school with 320 students who would directly benefit from the water. Now they don’t have to walk every day more than hour to collect water. Water would give them more time for school. It would also help the children and other villagers (about 700 other beneficiaries besides the children) with health due improved sanitation and hygiene.

Pastor & team have a great plan to sell farming products and with income support evangelists in unreached areas in nearby villages.

This is a good church and active in ministry. This area has lots of conflict and the church through evangelism can offer people real peace.

Thank you for your continued support.

‍

We distribute bread and food for the poor families in Moldova.

Your support is very big blessing for all the kids and adults who receive help.

May God richly reward all of you with His blessings!

You are in our prayers!

God bless you

fieldData.author: From our friends in Budapest
fieldData.description:
We have a little church in Tiszabecs, which is a village at the border. The church building is opened for the refugees who are coming from Ukraine. We collect food and money to meet their needs. We cook them meals and provide them basic supplies there, and help with transportation if needed. They can also spend the night there, if needed, and it has just been offered to us to use another building near the church as a temporary shelter for the people who are coming (the church is always full). There are a lot of children, so our volunteers entertain them as well.

Today we were able to take food to the other side of the border, to Transcarpathia, there are a lot of people in need also. We have volunteers who can help with medical service, there are a lot of children, and adults also who are really weak after arriving. A lot of people are coming, so your support is really appreciated.

Thank you for your continued support.
fieldData.name: Ukraine - Hungary

fieldData.author: From Jonathan
fieldData.description:
With a thankful heart I greet you from India. In our Orissa Center our children who come to our development center live in mud huts and hey are not guaranteed a solid meal each day at home. That's why we are there to care for them and provide fresh healthy food with High5's support.


This month we were able to provide a meal each day for 25 children and 5 adults each day. The menu is selected by the local staff we have who also operate Love Never Fails School where children use this nutritious food to fuel their minds to success to break poverty over their lives in Jesus' name. I listed the food in the text below the pics so you can see and know what they are eating.


Location: Golamunda, Orissa
fieldData.name: India
